name: Collection Operations Coordinator
summary: The headless coordinator that owns one collection and runs the WHOLE collection-operations pipeline — filter -> sort -> group -> **page** — over it, then hands the current page slice to the data-table for rendering and the totals to the pagination block. The two stage-owning blocks (data-table: filter/sort/group; pagination: page) each run one stage; until this existed no one ran the pipeline as a whole, so the obvious consumer wiring (page-then-render) sorted only the current page, not the whole book (surfaced by the loan-origination app, #317). This coordinator applies filter/sort/group across the entire set first (reusing the data-table's verified `applyPipeline`, whose `page` stage is a deliberate no-op), then windows the result — order is always collection-wide and the page is just the visible slice. Re-feeding the already-ordered slice back through the data-table is safe (filter/sort/group are idempotent on a processed slice). Ships a standalone `CollectionOperationsBehavior` and an optional headless `<collection-operations>` element that routes descendant `data-table-change` / `pagination-change` events and emits `collection-operations-change` — not a documented composition and not a rendered block (#452 ruling A/A).

wholeBookThenWindow.description: Run filter -> sort -> group across the ENTIRE collection, then apply the page stage (the slice) — never page-then-sort
wholeBookThenWindow.rationale: The intent contract is filter -> sort -> group -> page. Paging before sorting yields page-local order (the bug #317 surfaced). The coordinator owns the single collection so it can sequence the whole pipeline; pagination only windows the result.

standaloneHeadlessNotComposition.description: A standalone headless behavior + optional element wrapper, not a documented composition and not a rendered block
standaloneHeadlessNotComposition.rationale: Resolved in #452 (A/A): the cross-block coordination is real machinery (event wiring + the page stage), not prose a consumer hand-wires. It renders no DOM of its own — it coordinates the data-table + pagination blocks that do.

reuseApplyPipeline.description: Reuse the data-table's verified `applyPipeline` for filter/sort/group; add only the page stage + event wiring
reuseApplyPipeline.rationale: `applyPipeline`'s page stage is intentionally a no-op ('the Pagination block's job'). The coordinator supplies exactly that missing stage and the event routing, re-implementing none of the verified contract.

patterns: TanStack Table's single getRowModel pipeline (filter -> sort -> group -> paginate)
react.notes: TanStack runs all stages over one row model in order; we split the stages across composable blocks and add a coordinator that sequences them so order stays collection-wide.

intlCollator.usage: The headless filter → sort → group → page coordinator; locale-aware sort comparisons go through `Intl.Collator`.

arrayToSorted.usage: Sorting is non-mutating via `Array.prototype.toSorted`, leaving the source collection untouched.
